 FAMC21/2025 HKSAR v. WONG, CHUN LOK (é»ƒä¿Šæ¨‚)
 

 FAMC No. 21 of 2025
 

[2026] HKCFA 11

 IN THE COURT OF FINAL APPEAL OF THE
 

 HONG KONG SPECIAL ADMINISTRATIVE REGION
 

 MISCELLANEOUS PROCEEDINGS NO. 21 OF 2025 (CRIMINAL)
 

 (ON APPLICATION FOR LEAVE TO APPEAL FROM
 

 HCMA NO. 261 OF 2023)
 

 ________________________
 

 BETWEEN
 

 HKSAR
 

 Respondent
 

 and
 

 WONG CHUN LOK (é»ƒä¿Šæ¨‚)
 

 Applicant
 

 ________________________
 

 Appeal Committee:
 

 Mr Justice Ribeiro PJ, Mr Justice Fok PJ and Mr Justice Lam PJ
 

 Date of Hearing and Determination:
 

 24 March 2026
 

 ________________________
 

 DETERMINATION
 

 ________________________
 

 The Appeal Committee:
 

 1.
 
 We are satisfied that it is reasonably arguable that there has been substantial and grave injustice on the following grounds, namely:
 

 (a) That there was a failure properly to consider whether the mental element of the offences charged had been proved beyond reasonable doubt; and/or
 

 (b) That the judge wrongly refused to admit new evidence as to the defendant's medical condition.
 

 The appeal will be listed to be heard on 4 November 2026.
 

 (R A V Ribeiro)
 

 (Joseph Fok)
 

 (M H Lam)
 

 Permanent Judge
 

 Permanent Judge
 

 Permanent Judge
 

 Mr Simon So and Mr Jack Hui, instructed by L & L Lawyers, for the Applicant
 

 Ms Irene Poon, SPP and Mr Bryan Fung, PP, of the Department of Justice, for the Respondent
